LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1-3. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD THOSE CLAIMS ON GROUNDS THAT ARE INDEPENDENT FROM THE UNFAIR INSURANCE PRACTICES ACT (UIPA) (INS.C. 790.03). THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS CAUSE OF ACTION 4 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF CANNOT BRING THE CLAIM BASED ON THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN DEFENDANT AND ITS PARENT CORPORATION, AND THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN AND INSURER AND INSURED IS NOT A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP. SEE LOVE V. FIRE INS. EXCHANGE (1990) 221 CAL.APP. 3D 1136, 1147. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION 5 IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY OR BY ATTACHING THE CONTRACT TO PLEAD THE BREACH MORE CLEARLY. THE DEMURRER TO THE ELDER ABUSE CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD A CLAIM ON SOME VALID BASIS OTHER THAN INS.C. 781 OR B&P CODE 17206.1, NEITHER OF WHICH CAN SUPPORT THE CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 27, 2013 AT 9:30 A.M. THE REASON FOR THE CONTINUANCE IS THAT THE COURT STILL DOES NOT HAVE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION BRIEF THAT PLAINTIFFS FILED IN OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER. THE COURT NEEDS A COPY OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION BRIEF IN ORDER TO RULE ON THIS MOTION. PLAINTIFF MUST DROP OFF A COURTESY COPY OF ITS OPPOSITION BRIEF DIRECTLY TO DEPARTMENT 302, THE LAW AND MOTION COURTROOM (NOT THE CLERK'S OFFICE). THIS IS REQUIRED UNDER LOCAL RULE 2.6B. A COURTESY COPY MUST BE PROVIDED BY JUNE 20, 2013, BY 4:00 P.M., WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. (IN ADDITION, THE COURT WOULD APPRECIATE RECEIVING A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS FROM COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT, IF POSSIBLE.) COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS INSTRUCTED TO PROVIDE NOTICE OF THE CONTINUATION OF THE HEARING ON THE DEMURRER, AND AN ORDER THAT STATES THE SUBSTANCE OF THIS TENTATIVE RULING, AND TO BRING PROOF OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER TO THE HEARING ON JUNE 27, 2013. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 11, 2013 AT 9:30 A.M. DUE TO ERROR IN THE TENTATIVE RULING POSTED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN, AND DOES NOT STATE MATERIAL FACTS SHOWING DEFENDANT VIOLATED ANY LAWS. THE COMPLAINT DOES REFER TO CALIFORNIA INSURANCE CODE 790.03 AND BUS. & PROF. CODE 17200. HOWEVER, NO PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION EXISTS FOR VIOLATION OF THE UNFAIR INSURANCE PRACTICES ACT (UIPA), AND ENFORCEMENT POWERS ARE VESTED EXCLUSIVELY IN THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER. MORADI SHALAL V. FIREMAN'S FUND INS. COS. (1988), 46 CAL.3D 287, 304. THUS, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO STATE ALLEGATIONS NECESSARY TO PLEAD A CLAIM FOR VIOLATION OF THE UCL. PLAINTIFF MUST ALSO STATE FACTS NECESSARY TO PLEAD ELDER ABUSE. PLAINTIFF MUST CLEARLY PLEAD THE ESSENTIAL FACTS FOR EACH CAUSE OF ACTION, AS WELL AS STATE THE CAUSES OF ACTION ON WHICH PLAINTIFF SEEKS RELIEF. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Oct-21-2013 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to causes of action 1-3. Plaintiff has leave to plead those claims on grounds that are independent from the Unfair Insurance Practices Act (UIPA) (Ins.C. 790.03). The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as cause of action 4 because plaintiff cannot bring the claim based on the relationship between defendant and its parent corporation, and the relationship between and insurer and insured is not a fiduciary relationship. See Love v. Fire Ins. Exchange (1990) 221 Cal.App. 3d 1136, 1147. The demurrer to cause of action 5 is sustained with leave to amend to allege the terms of the contract with the requisite specificity or by attaching the contract to plead the breach more clearly. The demurrer to the elder abuse cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to plead a claim on some valid basis other than Ins.C. 781 or B&P Code 17206.1, neither of which can support the claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. The complaint is uncertain, and does not state material facts showing Defendant violated any laws. The complaint does refer to California Insurance Code 790.03 and Bus. & Prof. Code 17200. However, no private right of action exists for violation of the Unfair Insurance Practices Act (UIPA), and enforcement powers are vested exclusively in the Insurance Commissioner. Moradi Shalal v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Cos. (1988), 46 Cal.3d 287, 304. Thus, plaintiff is given leave to amend to state allegations necessary to plead a claim for violation of the UCL. Plaintiff must also state facts necessary to plead elder abuse. Plaintiff must clearly plead the essential facts for each cause of action, as well as state the causes of action on which Plaintiff seeks relief. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2013 Continued To Jun-27-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to June 27, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. The reason for the continuance is that the Court still does not have a courtesy copy of the opposition brief that Plaintiffs filed in opposition to the demurrer. The Court needs a copy of Plaintiff's opposition brief in order to rule on this motion. Plaintiff must drop off a courtesy copy of its opposition brief directly to Department 302, the Law and Motion courtroom (not the Clerk's office). This is required under Local Rule 2.6B. A courtesy copy must be provided by June 20, 2013, by 4:00 p.m., with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. (In addition, the Court would appreciate receiving a courtesy copy of the opposition papers from counsel for Defendant, if possible.) Counsel for Defendant is instructed to provide notice of the continuation of the hearing on the demurrer, and an order that states the substance of this tentative ruling, and to bring proof of service of this order to the hearing on June 27, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
